subtopics.title: Definition and Importance of Data Processing
common_misconceptions: Data and information are the same
Data processing is only done with computers
All data is useful without processing
subtopics.teaching_note: Key distinction: Data = raw, unorganized facts (numbers, text, images). Information = processed, organized data that is meaningful. Data processing = collection, recording, organizing, storage, retrieval, transformation, and presentation of data. Nigerian context: NPC (National Population Commission) processes census data, NBS (National Bureau of Statistics) processes economic data, WAEC processes examination results.

subtopics.title: Stages of Data Processing
common_misconceptions: Data processing is a linear process (it's cyclic)
subtopics.teaching_note: Data Processing Cycle: 1) Collection - gathering raw data (surveys, sensors). 2) Preparation - sorting, verifying, classifying. 3) Input - entering data into computer. 4) Processing - calculations, sorting, summarizing. 5) Output - results (reports, graphs). 6) Storage - saving for future use. 7) Distribution - sending to users. GIGO: Garbage In, Garbage Out - quality of output depends on quality of input. Feedback loop: output can become new input.

subtopics.title: Introduction to Spreadsheets
common_misconceptions: Excel and spreadsheets are different things
Formulas and functions are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Spreadsheet software: Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc. Cell reference: column letter + row number (A1, B2). Cell range: A1:C5. Formulas start with = sign. Common functions: SUM, AVERAGE, MAX, MIN, COUNT, IF. Relative reference: A1 (changes when copied). Absolute reference: $A$1 (stays fixed when copied). Mixed: $A1 or A$1. Charts: column, bar, pie, line. Nigerian application: budgeting, payroll, student records, sales tracking.
